title: wibta if i don’t invite my friend to my grad party?
text: i am having a grad school graduation party with friends and family to be invited. i have one “friend” i have been trying to phase out of my life as her inappropriate choices of conversation and vulgar language are inappropriate for most places, and she’s what i will refer to as a “joy stealer”. she is not the type of person that can be happy for somebody else and always has a negative comment to make about anything. for these reasons i don’t want to invite her to the party. i am quite confident she would have negative comments to make and would definitely act in a manner that would offend some of my family members. for some additional background we’ve been friends for over 10 years, i’ve recently started to phase her out of my life as i feel with maturity we have grown apart. i don’t think she’s even aware i’m trying to do this. with so many mutual friends i still see her quite often. she has asked if i was planning a grad party, and i do believe she would find out through the grapevine. so my question is, wibta if i i don’t choose to invite her for reasons mentioned above? on one hand, we have a lot of history together and i don’t want to hurt her feelings nor do i think she would understand the lack of invite, on the other hand, this was a big accomplishment for me and i want to celebrate without having to babysit anybody’s behavior.

storyboard.content: I am having a grad school graduation party with friends and family invited. We’ve been friends for over 10 years, but I’ve recently started to phase her out of my life as I feel we have grown apart with maturity.

My friend uses inappropriate language and vulgar conversation that is unsuitable for most settings. She is not the type of person who can be happy for someone else and always has a negative comment to make.
I don’t want to invite her to my graduation party.
I am quite confident she would make negative comments and act in a manner that would offend some of my family members.
This was a big accomplishment for me and I want to celebrate without having to babysit anybody’s behavior.
She has asked me if I was planning a grad party, and I believe she would find out about it through our mutual friends.
I don’t think she’s even aware I’m trying to phase her out, and I don’t want to hurt her feelings. I don’t think she would understand the lack of an invite.
